scope.summary: Implement post-simulation all-quad mesh re-topology using the isotropic remeshing approach (pair adjacent triangles to quads along shortest diagonal) in tailor-solver, producing a quad mesh that preserves UV island boundaries and grain direction, exported alongside the triangle mesh in glTF/USD
acceptance_criteria: A remesh_to_quads(mesh: &SolverMesh, options: QuadRemeshOptions) -> QuadMesh function is implemented in tailor-solver/src/mesh.rs
QuadMesh preserves all UV island boundaries: no quad spans across a seam cut
QuadMesh preserves grain direction: panel grain angle is not distorted by the re-topology pass
The resulting QuadMesh is exportable as a separate glTF mesh primitive with TRIANGLES topology derived from quad diagonal split (fallback for non-quad-native importers)
tailor_generate_quad_mesh MCP tool triggers quad re-topology for a given garment_id and simulation_run_id, stores the result as an artifact, and emits a TailorQuadMeshGenerated EventLedger event
An integration test: a 4-panel garment re-meshed to quads has zero quads crossing UV seam cuts
scope.risk_if_missed: Without all-quad output, garments imported into Blender or ZBrush for subdivision or sculpting detail show triangle artifacts on curved surfaces (shoulders, bust, drape). Subdivision on triangles produces pinching; DCC artists must manually retopologize every exported garment, which is the single most time-consuming step in the cloth-to-game pipeline.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
